annotations.label: Regular polytope

anchor.snippet: a regular polytope is a polytope whose symmetry group acts transitively on its flags

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of polytope nor flag-transitive symmetry of one; only `Affine.Simplex` and `Polygon` exist.

annotations.label: Classical regular polytope (facets and vertex figures)

anchor.snippet: a regular polytope in n dimensions may be defined as having regular facets

annotations.note: No notion of facets/vertex figures or regular polytopes exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol of a regular polytope

anchor.snippet: A regular polytope can be represented by a Schläfli symbol of the form

annotations.note: Grep for `Schläfli`/`Schlafli` yields no results in Mathlib.

annotations.label: One-dimensional regular polytope

anchor.snippet: the line segment simultaneously serves as the 1-simplex, the 1-hypercube and the 1-orthoplex

annotations.note: Mathlib does not identify 1-simplex/1-cube/1-orthoplex as a common line segment; no regular polytope framework.

annotations.label: Two-dimensional regular polygons

anchor.snippet: there are infinitely many regular polygons

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Polygon` but no definition of regular polygon and no enumeration result.

annotations.label: Higher-dimensional regular polytopes (5+)

anchor.snippet: the simplex, hypercube, and orthoplex are the only regular polytopes

annotations.note: No classification theorem for regular polytopes exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual regular polytopes

anchor.snippet: Two distinct regular polytopes with the same symmetry are dual to one another

annotations.note: No notion of dual polytope is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol for convex regular polygon

anchor.snippet: A convex regular polygon having

annotations.note: Schläfli symbols are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol for star polygon

anchor.snippet: A regular

annotations.note: No star polygon or Schläfli notation is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol for regular polyhedron

anchor.snippet: A regular polyhedron having faces

annotations.note: Mathlib has no regular polyhedron concept or Schläfli notation.

annotations.label: Nine regular polyhedra

anchor.snippet: The nine regular polyhedra are the five Platonic solids

annotations.note: Grep for `Platonic`/`Kepler`/`Poinsot` returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol for regular 4-polytope

anchor.snippet: A regular 4-polytope having cells

annotations.note: No 4-polytope or Schläfli notation in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol for regular 5-polytope

anchor.snippet: A regular 5-polytope is denoted by

annotations.note: No 5-polytope or Schläfli notation in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual of a regular polytope is regular

anchor.snippet: The dual of a regular polytope is also a regular polytope

annotations.note: Polytope duality is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex figure as dual of dual's facet

anchor.snippet: The vertex figure of a regular polytope is the dual of the dual polytope's facet

annotations.note: Vertex figures and polytope duality are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Measure and cross polytopes are dual

anchor.snippet: The measure and cross polytopes in any dimension are dual to each other

annotations.note: Hypercubes and orthoplexes as polytopes (and their duality) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Self-dual regular polytopes (palindromic symbols)

anchor.snippet: If the Schläfli symbol is palindromic

annotations.note: Schläfli symbols and polytope duality are absent in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simplex

anchor.snippet: The simplex is a generalization of the notion of a triangle or tetrahedron to arbitrary dimensions

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Affine.Simplex k P n` as `n+1` affinely independent points, generalizing the geometric simplex but without a regularity condition.

annotations.label: Low-dimensional simplices

anchor.snippet: a 0-dimensional simplex: point

annotations.note: Low-dimensional simplices (point, edge, triangle) are instances of `Affine.Simplex`; `Affine.Triangle` is defined explicitly.

annotations.label: Hypercubes (measure polytopes)

anchor.snippet: These are the measure polytopes or hypercubes

annotations.note: No hypercube polytope is defined in Mathlib (only unrelated `Combinatorics.HalesJewett` hypercube combinatorics).

annotations.label: Number of vertices of n-cube

anchor.snippet: An n -cube has 2 n vertices

annotations.note: No `n`-cube polytope exists in Mathlib to count vertices of.

annotations.label: Construction of hypercubes

anchor.snippet: The process of making each hypercube can be visualised on a graph

annotations.note: Iterative geometric hypercube construction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthoplexes (cross polytopes)

anchor.snippet: These are the cross polytopes or orthoplexes

annotations.note: Grep for `orthoplex`/`cross polytope` yields no Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Number of vertices of n-orthoplex

anchor.snippet: An n -orthoplex has 2n vertices

annotations.note: No orthoplex is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Construction of orthoplexes

anchor.snippet: The process of making each orthoplex can be visualised on a graph

annotations.note: Iterative orthoplex construction is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of regular polytopes via Coxeter groups

anchor.snippet: Regular polytopes are in bijection with Coxeter groups with linear Coxeter-Dynkin diagram

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `CoxeterMatrix`/`CoxeterSystem` (and types A,B,...), but has no regular polytopes nor any bijection with linear diagrams.

annotations.label: Bijection between regular polytopes and linear Coxeter diagrams

anchor.snippet: The bijection between regular polytopes and Coxeter groups with linear Coxeter-Dynkin diagram can be understood as follows

annotations.note: No connection between Coxeter systems and polytopes is formalized.

annotations.label: Kepler-Poinsot polyhedra classification

anchor.snippet: Augustin Cauchy proved the list complete in 1812

annotations.note: Kepler–Poinsot polyhedra are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Six regular convex 4-polytopes

anchor.snippet: Schläfli showed that there are six regular convex polytopes in 4 dimensions

annotations.note: No 4-polytope classification exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three regular polytopes in dimensions ≥ 5

anchor.snippet: there are exactly three finite regular polytopes

annotations.note: Higher-dimensional regular polytope classification is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular polygon

anchor.snippet: A regular polygon is a polygon whose edges are all equal and whose angles are all equal

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Polygon` has no `IsRegular` predicate; grep for `regularPolygon`/`RegularPolygon` returns nothing.

annotations.label: Regular polyhedron

anchor.snippet: A regular polyhedron is a polyhedron whose faces are all congruent regular polygons

annotations.note: No polyhedron type, let alone a regularity predicate, is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular n-polytope (recursive)

anchor.snippet: a regular n -polytope is an n -dimensional polytope whose

annotations.note: No polytope type or recursive regularity exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular n-polytope (flag-transitivity)

anchor.snippet: An n -polytope is regular if any set consisting of a vertex, an edge containing it

annotations.note: No notion of flag or flag-transitive action on polytopes exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flag and succinct regularity

anchor.snippet: A regular polytope is one whose symmetry group is transitive on its flags

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define flags of polytopes nor transitivity on them.

annotations.label: Regular skew polyhedra of Coxeter and Petrie

anchor.snippet: Coxeter and Petrie discovered three infinite structures

annotations.note: Skew polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular apeirotope

anchor.snippet: he called regular apeirotopes

annotations.note: Apeirotopes are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Skew apeirogon (zig-zag)

anchor.snippet: A simple example of a skew apeirogon would be a zig-zag

annotations.note: Skew apeirogons are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex polytope

anchor.snippet: A polytope constructed in such a unitary space is called a complex polytope

annotations.note: Complex polytopes in unitary spaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: 11-cell with hemi-icosahedral facets

anchor.snippet: Grünbaum also discovered the 11-cell

annotations.note: The 11-cell and hemi-icosahedron are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hemicube

anchor.snippet: the relationship of the cube and the hemicube

annotations.note: No hemicube construction in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 11-cell requires elliptic hyperspace

anchor.snippet: The 11-cell cannot be formed with regular geometry in flat (Euclidean) hyperspace

annotations.note: Result about the 11-cell is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abstract polytope

anchor.snippet: An abstract polytope is defined as a partially ordered set

annotations.note: No abstract polytope poset is defined; Mathlib has `Order.Grade` graded orders but no axiomatic polytope structure.

annotations.label: Geometric polytope as realization

anchor.snippet: A geometric polytope is understood to be a realization of the abstract polytope

annotations.note: Without abstract polytopes, there is no realization notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: All polygons are abstractly regular

anchor.snippet: All polygons are regular in the abstract world

annotations.note: Abstract regularity of polygons is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vertex figure of an abstract polytope

anchor.snippet: The vertex figure of a given abstract n -polytope at a given vertex

annotations.note: Vertex figures are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square pyramid as non-regular with regular facets and vertex figures

anchor.snippet: for example, the square pyramid

annotations.note: Square pyramid as a polytope example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classical vertex figure realises abstract one

anchor.snippet: The classical vertex figure will, however, be a realisation of the abstract one

annotations.note: No vertex figure realization theory exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-constructible regular polygons

anchor.snippet: The simplest few regular polygons that are impossible to construct

annotations.note: Gauss–Wantzel-style constructibility results for regular polygons are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Number of fold-out nets for cube and dodecahedron

anchor.snippet: there are 11 for the cube, and over 900000 for the dodecahedron

annotations.note: Counts of polyhedron nets are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Intersection of regular polytope with hyperplane

anchor.snippet: The intersection of a four-dimensional (or higher) regular polytope with a three-dimensional hyperplane will be a polytope

annotations.note: Cross-sections of regular polytopes by hyperplanes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Abstract regular polytope from symmetry group

anchor.snippet: an important theorem in the study of abstract regular polytopes, providing a technique that allows the abstract regular polytope to be constructed from its symmetry group

annotations.note: Reconstruction of abstract regular polytopes from symmetry groups is absent from Mathlib.
